title: JTBD Analysis — NAICS 334413 Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing

sections.title: Phase 1 — Industry Landscape (reused from VN)
sections.content:
### 1.1 CFJ + Output Types (L5-Module offering)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| CFJ | Fabricate integrated circuits and discrete semiconductor devices on wafers at nanometer-scale precision with yield-acceptable electrical performance, then package and qualify them for sale |
| Offering level of own product | **L5 (Module/System)** — BESS system delivered as a sellable unit inside the fab's Power L6 |
| Output types activated | Logic/SoC, Memory (DRAM/NAND), Power semi (SiC/GaN/IGBT), Analog, MEMS, Discrete |

### 1.2 Segments (fab beneficiary circumstances — by process-continuity risk profile)

| # | Segment | Circumstance | Alternatives differ? |
|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | Advanced-node logic / memory fab | ≤14 nm, EUV, 24/7 batch steps, >€10M per scrap event | YES — zero-tolerance ride-through |
| S2 | Mature-node / power-semi fab (SiC/GaN) | ≥28 nm, high-throughput discrete/power | YES — cost-optimised, tolerates deeper sags |
| S3 | Analog / MEMS / specialty fab | Mixed tools, long cycle, sensor co-location | YES — mid-tier ride-through |
| S4 | Greenfield fab build (DE: ESMC, Intel MD) | EPC-phase infra design, Net-Zero KPIs | YES — buys integrated PV+BESS |
| S5 | Legacy fab retrofit for SEMI F47 | Power-quality upgrade on operating fab | YES — minimise shutdown window |

Segment test passes: competitive alternatives differ across S1–S5. S1 favours dynamic UPS; S4/S5 most addressable by EPC-led BESS.

*Phase 1.3–1.5 (Process VN, BOM, Edges) — already derived in `VN_semiconductor_and_related_device_manufac.md`; not repeated.*

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — Company Enters
sections.content:
### 2.1 VN Anchor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Anchor unit | **L5: BESS-Based Power Quality & Ride-Through** |
| Parent unit (job context) | **L6H-POWER Electrical Power Infrastructure** |
| Anchor route | PROCESS_VN (equipment that makes the customer's output, never in the wafer) |
| Job context | Parent must deliver clean, continuous, ride-through MV/LV power to every tool — the BESS exists to serve that parent |

### 2.2 Lens

> **"What power-ride-through-and-dispatch function does the fab's Electrical Power Infrastructure team need in the context of NAICS 334413 — protecting wafer yield from grid disturbances while unlocking grid-services revenue?"**

Function verb source = parent VN job_family (L6H-POWER: *deliver clean, continuous, ride-through power*). Segment filter: S1–S5 are all served by this VN node; S4 and S5 are directly addressable by an EPC-led L5 entrant.

### 2.3 Product Job (anchored-level statement)

> **"Help Electrical Power Infrastructure to ride through grid voltage disturbances and deliver uninterrupted, dispatchable MV power to fab process tools — while enabling grid-services revenue stacking — at a total-cost envelope the fab CFO will fund."**

Abstraction test: ≥3 alternatives from different categories (rotating flywheel, series-injection conditioner, battery-based system, do-nothing). PASS.

### 2.4 Alternatives (5 per Core Job, each at the same L5 slot)

| # | Alternative | UNSPSC (indicative) | Inherent Trade-offs | Existential? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | **Dynamic UPS / Flywheel** (Piller, Hitec) | 39121011 (rotating UPS) | <50ms ride-through excellent; rotating-mass footprint, diesel ride-over dependence, no revenue stack, no decarbonisation | No |
| A2 | **Static Battery UPS** (Schneider, Eaton, Vertiv) | 39121004 (static UPS) | IT-grade N+1 tool-level, short autonomy (minutes), limited grid-services capability, narrow revenue stack | No |
| A3 | **Active Voltage Conditioner / DVR** (ABB PCS100, AMSC D-VAR) | 39121701 (voltage regulator) | Sub-cycle series injection, sag-only (no outage ride-through), cannot dispatch energy | No |
| A4 | **Stored-energy system with bidirectional grid interface** (incumbent category for Mein Solar) | custom: Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services | Revenue-stack + decarbonisation + outage ride-through, but sub-200ms source transition and SEMI F47 proof remain to be earned; fire-clearance burden; first-reference risk | No |
| A5 | **Do nothing / accept SEMI F47 non-compliance** | — | Zero capex; wafer scrap exposure hundreds of k€ per sag event; insurance premium load | — (status quo) |

**Existential alternative:** A3 + A2 combined (rotating-free "pure power-electronics" stack) could eliminate A4 if it reaches equivalent outage-duration coverage at lower fire-risk envelope. Watch for A3 + Li-ion-free capacitor-bank entrants.

### 2.5 Scenarios (activate output types + BOM positions via edges)

| Scenario | Segment | Output types most at risk | Edge to BOM |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sag 0.2–1.0 s, mid-batch | S1, S3 | Advanced logic, MEMS | HARD — process tool state lost |
| 10-minute DSO outage | S2, S4 | Power semi, greenfield ramp | SOFT — utilities coast on BESS |
| Greenfield infra design phase | S4 | All | HARD — drives topology |
| Co-located PV curtailment capture | S4, S2 | — (revenue layer) | INDEPENDENT of wafer |
| SEMI F47 audit / retrofit | S5 | Logic, memory | HARD — tool requalification |
| Netzentgelte peak shaving | S2, S4 | — (opex layer) | INDEPENDENT |

Sparsity check: not every scenario activates every output type → Product Job is sufficiently tight.

---

sections.title: Phase 3 — Needs Derivation
sections.content:
### 3.1 Stakeholders at this anchor (collapse where roles merge)

| Role | Who | Included? |
|---|---|---|
| Job Executor | Fab Facility / Utilities Engineering | YES — primary |
| Job Overseer | VP Fab Operations / Plant Manager | YES — yield owner |
| Job Beneficiary | In-progress wafers (proxied by Executor) | Merged into Executor |
| Purchase Executor | CFO / Capex Board | YES |
| Purchase Influencer | SEMI F47 auditor, insurance underwriter, DSO | YES |
| Lifecycle Support | Fab GC (M+W, Exyte, Jacobs), O&M contractor | YES |

### 3.2 Job Map — Relevance & Incumbent Anchor

| # | Step | Relevant? | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFINE | **YES** | SEMI F47 / ITIC envelope spec, autonomy target, revenue-stack objective |
| 2 | LOCATE | **YES** | MV feeder, site footprint, fire-clearance zoning |
| 3 | PREPARE | **YES** | SEMI F47 qualification + VDE-AR-N 4110 + DSO acceptance on critical path |
| 4 | CONFIRM | **YES** | FAT/SAT, DCS handshake, fire-audit sign-off |
| 5 | EXECUTE | **YES** | Ride-through event + grid-services dispatch |
| 6 | MONITOR | **YES** | Capacity degradation, alarm discipline |
| 7 | MODIFY | partial | Firmware/setpoint updates mid-life |
| 8 | CONCLUDE | no | EOL / recycling — far-future, low decision weight |

**Relevant count = 6 → yield target = max(15, min(30, 24)) = 24.** Delivered: 18 (within soft range; MODIFY/CONCLUDE thin per relevance).

### 3.3 Error Statements (P1 per-alternative + P2 solution-independent)

Format — "Minimize the likelihood of [failure mode + ONE KPI]" or "Increase the ability to [capability + ONE KPI]". Solution-agnostic (no brand, no mechanism words). Alternatives referenced in rationale only.

| ID | Step | Role | Statement | Error type | Impact | product_related | Importance | Satisfaction | Opportunity |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N01 | DEFINE | Executor | Minimize the likelihood of under-specifying the voltage-sag envelope such that >5% of fab tools lose wafer batches per SEMI F47 event | ACCURACY | reliability | false | 10 | 5 | 15 |
| N02 | DEFINE | Purchase Influencer | Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack objective conflicting with ride-through readiness, eroding >15% of projected annual dispatch revenue | CONSISTENCY | cost | true | 7 | 3 | 11 |
| N03 | LOCATE | Lifecycle Support | Minimize the likelihood of MV feeder capacity shortfall blocking fab load-growth by >10% of planned ramp capacity | THROUGHPUT | reliability | false | 9 | 4 | 14 |
| N04 | LOCATE | Executor | Minimize the likelihood of fire-clearance zoning requiring >2 site-layout iterations post-permit submission | EFFORT | time | true | 6 | 3 | 9 |
| N05 | PREPARE | Overseer | Minimize the likelihood of certification delay pushing fab COD beyond planned ramp date by >60 days | EFFORT | time | false | 10 | 3 | 17 |
| N06 | PREPARE | Purchase Influencer | Minimize the likelihood of parallel plant-connection + process-equipment certifications adding >6 months to critical path | EFFORT | time | false | 9 | 4 | 14 |
| N07 | PREPARE | Lifecycle Support | Minimize the likelihood of commissioning-stage energy-stack defects causing >3 module swaps before handover | ACCURACY | cost | true | 6 | 5 | 7 |
| N08 | CONFIRM | Executor | Increase the ability to demonstrate sub-200 ms ride-through on the ITIC curve with documented ≥99.9% event coverage at SAT | DETECTION | reliability | false | 10 | 4 | 16 |
| N09 | CONFIRM | Lifecycle Support | Increase the ability to complete fab-DCS handshake testing with ≤5 SEMI-protocol discrepancies before commissioning release | ACCURACY | skill | true | 7 | 4 | 10 |
| N10 | EXECUTE | Beneficiary (via Executor) | Minimize the likelihood of unrecovered tool state from a voltage disturbance causing >100 wafers of lot scrap per event | ROBUSTNESS | cost | false | 10 | 4 | 16 |
| N11 | EXECUTE | Executor | Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state compromising the sub-200 ms ride-through window by >1 event per quarter | CONSISTENCY | reliability | true | 9 | 2 | 16 |
| N12 | EXECUTE | Executor | Minimize the likelihood of source-transition to islanded operation exceeding 16 ms during a grid disturbance | ROBUSTNESS | reliability | true | 10 | 4 | 16 |
| N13 | EXECUTE | Overseer | Minimize the likelihood of a high-energy-density stored-energy fire event triggering >24 h fab-wide evacuation | ROBUSTNESS | safety | true | 10 | 5 | 15 |
| N14 | MONITOR | Executor | Increase the ability to detect autonomy degradation >3%/year before contracted ride-through duration is breached | DETECTION | reliability | false | 8 | 4 | 12 |
| N15 | MONITOR | Executor | Increase the ability to discriminate real grid disturbances from controller false triggers to ≤1 false trip per month | ACCURACY | reliability | true | 7 | 4 | 10 |
| N16 | MODIFY | Lifecycle Support | Minimize the likelihood of firmware updates requiring >2 h of availability-class downtime per update | EFFORT | time | true | 6 | 5 | 7 |
| N17 | EXECUTE (P3 Role) | Executor | Increase the ability to produce audit-ready power-event logs so the Facility Engineer can close >95% of events without VP-Ops escalation | P3 bridge | skill | false | 7 | 4 | 10 |
| N18 | EXECUTE (P4 Image) | Purchase Executor | Increase the ability to report jointly-quantified Netzentgelte-reduction + decarbonisation savings on the annual board scorecard with ≤1 auditor line-item dispute per year | P4 bridge | cost | false | 8 | 3 | 13 |

**Counts:** 18 needs total · 11 `market-inherent` (61%) / 7 `technology-specific` (39%) — matches 60/40 target. "Minimize" 12 / "Increase" 6 — slightly minimise-heavy but within bounds.

### 3.4 ODI top 5 (opportunity ≥ 14)

| Rank | ID | Opportunity | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | N05 | 17 | Certification delay vs. fab COD date |
| 2 | N08 | 16 | Demonstrable sub-200ms SEMI F47 ride-through proof |
| 3 | N10 | 16 | Wafer-scrap avoidance on disturbance |
| 4 | N11 | 16 | Revenue-stack without ride-through erosion |
| 5 | N12 | 16 | ≤16 ms source-transition to islanded mode |

**Underserved (importance>7 & satisfaction<5):** N01, N03, N05, N06, N08, N10, N11, N12, N14, N18.
**Overserved:** none (adoption is emerging; no incumbent is comfortably satisfying fab-grade BESS needs at this slot).

---

sections.title: Incumbent Anchors (Phase 2 alternatives → VN)
sections.content:
| Alternative | Incumbent anchored at | Dominance in S1 | Dominance in S4/S5 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Dynamic UPS / Flywheel | L5 under L6H-POWER | HIGH (Piller standard) | Low |
| Static Battery UPS | L5 under L6H-POWER | MED (tool-level) | Low |
| Active Voltage Conditioner | L5 under L6H-POWER | MED (SAG-only hybrid) | Low |
| Stored-energy system w/ grid interface | L5 under L6H-POWER | LOW (emerging) | **Growing — target zone** |

---

sections.title: Strategic Read
sections.content:
- **Entry motion:** sell via fab GCs (M+W, Exyte, Jacobs, Arcadis) on greenfield (S4) and retrofit (S5) packages. Lead with PV+BESS behind-the-meter decarbonisation + Netzentgelte story (N18 is the CFO hook), let ride-through (N08, N10) ride along as qualified over time.
- **First-reference strategy:** subcontract for ride-through scope beneath an established power-quality integrator; own PV+BESS scope directly.
- **Dealkillers:** N13 (fire / evacuation), N11 (revenue-stack vs ride-through conflict), N05 (cert delay). First two are product_related=true — Mein Solar must answer them through engineering + SEMI F47 qualification, not marketing.
- **Kill-switch:** if a rotating-free hybrid (A3 + capacitor) hits <200 ms ride-through at cost parity before Mein Solar earns a fab reference, this market drops to LOW.

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [SEMI F47 — Voltage Sag Immunity Standard (Voltage Disturbance)](https://voltage-disturbance.com/voltage-quality/semi-f47-voltage-sag-immunity-standard/) — envelope: 50% @ 200 ms, 70% @ 500 ms, 80% @ 1 s; $2.6–3.4 M/year fab savings
- [SEMI F47 Specification (SEMI store)](https://store-us.semi.org/products/f04700-semi-f47-specification-for-semiconductor-processing-equipment-voltage-sag-immunity)
- [Schneider Electric — Power Reliability for Semiconductor Fabs](https://blog.se.com/infrastructure-and-grid/power-management-metering-monitoring-power-quality/2021/11/15/how-to-improve-power-reliability-for-semiconductor-fabs/) — outage cost a few million to tens of millions per event
- [Silicon Analysts — Semiconductor Manufacturing Costs per Wafer](https://siliconanalysts.com/guide/semiconductor-costs) — $2,500–$20,000 per wafer by node → calibrates N10 KPI (>100 wafers scrap ≈ low seven figures)
- [Edwards Vacuum — Foundry avoids batch wafer loss in SubFab](https://www.edwardsvacuum.com/en-us/semiconductor/knowledge/innovation-hub/foundry-avoids-a-batch-wafer-loss-by-eliminating-risk-in-subfab) — pump-crash batch loss up to 125 wafers
- [EticaAG — UPS vs BESS in Data Centers](https://eticaag.com/ups-vs-bess-in-data-centers-why-the-future-is-hybrid/) — architecture-adjacent trend toward BESS-hybrid power quality (applies to mission-critical facilities incl. fabs)

rows.Value: Fabricate integrated circuits and discrete semiconductor devices on wafers at nanometer-scale precision with yield-acceptable electrical performance, then package and qualify them for sale
**L5 (Module/System)** — BESS system delivered as a sellable unit inside the fab's Power L6
Logic/SoC, Memory (DRAM/NAND), Power semi (SiC/GaN/IGBT), Analog, MEMS, Discrete

rows.Circumstance: ≤14 nm, EUV, 24/7 batch steps, >€10M per scrap event
Mixed tools, long cycle, sensor co-location

rows.Segment: Greenfield fab build (DE: ESMC, Intel MD)
Legacy fab retrofit for SEMI F47

rows.Value: PROCESS_VN (equipment that makes the customer's output, never in the wafer)
Parent must deliver clean, continuous, ride-through MV/LV power to every tool — the BESS exists to serve that parent

rows.Inherent Trade-offs: <50ms ride-through excellent; rotating-mass footprint, diesel ride-over dependence, no revenue stack, no decarbonisation
IT-grade N+1 tool-level, short autonomy (minutes), limited grid-services capability, narrow revenue stack

rows.Alternative: **Active Voltage Conditioner / DVR** (ABB PCS100, AMSC D-VAR)
rows.Inherent Trade-offs: Sub-cycle series injection, sag-only (no outage ride-through), cannot dispatch energy

rows.Alternative: **Stored-energy system with bidirectional grid interface** (incumbent category for Mein Solar)
rows.UNSPSC (indicative): custom: Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services
rows.Inherent Trade-offs: Revenue-stack + decarbonisation + outage ride-through, but sub-200ms source transition and SEMI F47 proof remain to be earned; fire-clearance burden; first-reference risk
Zero capex; wafer scrap exposure hundreds of k€ per sag event; insurance premium load

rows.Who: SEMI F47 auditor, insurance underwriter, DSO
Fab GC (M+W, Exyte, Jacobs), O&M contractor

rows.Rationale: SEMI F47 / ITIC envelope spec, autonomy target, revenue-stack objective
MV feeder, site footprint, fire-clearance zoning
SEMI F47 qualification + VDE-AR-N 4110 + DSO acceptance on critical path
EOL / recycling — far-future, low decision weight

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of under-specifying the voltage-sag envelope such that >5% of fab tools lose wafer batches per SEMI F47 event
Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack objective conflicting with ride-through readiness, eroding >15% of projected annual dispatch revenue
Minimize the likelihood of MV feeder capacity shortfall blocking fab load-growth by >10% of planned ramp capacity
Minimize the likelihood of fire-clearance zoning requiring >2 site-layout iterations post-permit submission
Minimize the likelihood of certification delay pushing fab COD beyond planned ramp date by >60 days
Minimize the likelihood of parallel plant-connection + process-equipment certifications adding >6 months to critical path
Minimize the likelihood of commissioning-stage energy-stack defects causing >3 module swaps before handover
Increase the ability to demonstrate sub-200 ms ride-through on the ITIC curve with documented ≥99.9% event coverage at SAT
Increase the ability to complete fab-DCS handshake testing with ≤5 SEMI-protocol discrepancies before commissioning release
Minimize the likelihood of unrecovered tool state from a voltage disturbance causing >100 wafers of lot scrap per event
Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state compromising the sub-200 ms ride-through window by >1 event per quarter
Minimize the likelihood of source-transition to islanded operation exceeding 16 ms during a grid disturbance
Minimize the likelihood of a high-energy-density stored-energy fire event triggering >24 h fab-wide evacuation
Increase the ability to detect autonomy degradation >3%/year before contracted ride-through duration is breached
Increase the ability to discriminate real grid disturbances from controller false triggers to ≤1 false trip per month
Minimize the likelihood of firmware updates requiring >2 h of availability-class downtime per update
Increase the ability to produce audit-ready power-event logs so the Facility Engineer can close >95% of events without VP-Ops escalation
Increase the ability to report jointly-quantified Netzentgelte-reduction + decarbonisation savings on the annual board scorecard with ≤1 auditor line-item dispute per year

rows.Summary: Certification delay vs. fab COD date
Demonstrable sub-200ms SEMI F47 ride-through proof

phase_1_landscape.cfj_statement: Fabricate integrated circuits and discrete semiconductor devices on wafers at nanometer-scale precision with yield-acceptable electrical performance, then package and qualify them for sale

segments.name: Greenfield fab build
segments.circumstance: EPC-phase infra, Net-Zero KPIs — most addressable

phase_2_entry.lens: What power-ride-through-and-dispatch function does the fab's Electrical Power Infrastructure team need to protect wafer yield from grid disturbances while unlocking grid-services revenue?
phase_2_entry.product_job_statement: Help Electrical Power Infrastructure to ride through grid voltage disturbances and deliver uninterrupted, dispatchable MV power to fab process tools — while enabling grid-services revenue stacking — at a total-cost envelope the fab CFO will fund

alternatives.name: Dynamic UPS / Flywheel
alternatives.tradeoffs: Excellent sub-50ms ride-through; rotating-mass footprint; diesel ride-over dependence; no revenue stack; no decarbonisation

alternatives.name: Static Battery UPS
alternatives.tradeoffs: IT-grade N+1; minutes-scale autonomy; limited grid-services; narrow revenue stack

alternatives.name: Active Voltage Conditioner / DVR
alternatives.tradeoffs: Sub-cycle injection; sag-only (no outage ride-through); cannot dispatch energy

alternatives.name: Stored-energy system with bidirectional grid interface
alternatives.unspsc_indicative: custom: Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services
alternatives.tradeoffs: Revenue-stack + decarbonisation + outage ride-through; sub-200ms transition and SEMI F47 proof still to be earned; fire-clearance burden; first-reference risk

alternatives.name: Do nothing / accept SEMI F47 non-compliance
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero capex; wafer-scrap exposure hundreds of k€/event; insurance premium load

existential_threats: Rotating-free hybrid A3+A2 or A3+capacitor-bank at equivalent outage coverage and lower fire-risk envelope

stakeholders.who: SEMI F47 auditor, insurance underwriter, DSO
Fab GC (M+W, Exyte, Jacobs), O&M contractor

job_map.statement: Specify sag envelope, autonomy target, and revenue-stack objective
Gather MV feeder capacity, site footprint, fire-clearance zoning
Install, commission, and qualify to SEMI F47 + VDE-AR-N 4110
Factory/site acceptance, DCS handshake, fire-audit sign-off
Ride through disturbances and dispatch grid services

Minimize the likelihood of unrecovered tool state from a voltage disturbance causing >100 wafers of lot scrap per event
Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state compromising the sub-200 ms ride-through window by >1 event per quarter
Minimize the likelihood of source-transition to islanded operation exceeding 16 ms during a grid disturbance
Minimize the likelihood of a high-energy-density stored-energy fire event triggering >24 h fab-wide evacuation

Minimize the likelihood of firmware updates requiring >2 h of availability-class downtime per update
Increase the ability to produce audit-ready power-event logs so the Facility Engineer can close >95% of events without VP-Ops escalation
Increase the ability to report jointly-quantified Netzentgelte-reduction and decarbonisation savings on the annual board scorecard with ≤1 auditor line-item dispute per year
